<content>
1: # Just-OC: OpenCode/Kilo Mono Repo
2: 
3: Unified OpenCode and Kilo Code configuration repository for managing plugins, agents, skills, and commands in a mono repo structure.
4: 
5: ## Quick Start
6: 
7: ```bash
8: # Clone this repository
9: git clone <repo-url> ~/just-oc
10: 
11: # Symlink for OpenCode
12: ln -s ~/just-oc/.opencode ~/.config/opencode
13: 
14: # Symlink for Kilo Code
15: ln -s ~/just-oc/.opencode ~/.config/kilo/opencode
16: # OR
17: ln -s ~/just-oc/.opencode ~/.kilo/opencode
18: 
19: # Restart OpenCode/Kilo to load the new configuration
20: ```
21: 
22: ## Directory Structure
23: 
24: | Directory | Purpose |
25: |-----------|---------|
26: | `.opencode/agents/` | Custom agent definitions |
27: | `.opencode/commands/` | Custom slash commands |
28: | `.opencode/skills/` | Agent skills (api-design, code-review, etc.) |
29: | `.opencode/config/` | Shared configuration snippets |
30: | `.kilocode/skills/` | Kilo-specific skills |
31: | `plugins/` | Plugin source code repositories |
32: 
33: ## Features
34: 
35: - **Unified Configuration**: Single `.opencode/` directory works for both OpenCode and Kilo
36: - **Mono Repo Structure**: All plugins and configs in one place
37: - **Symlink-Based**: Easy system integration without copying files
38: - **Extensible**: Add new agents, skills, and commands by adding files
39: 
40: ## Managing Configuration
41: 
42: ### Adding a Custom Agent
43: 
44: ```bash
45: # Create agent file
46: cat > .opencode/agents/my-agent.md << 'AGENT_EOF'
47: ---
48: description: My custom agent
49: mode: subagent
50: permission:
51:   edit: deny
52:   bash: ask
53: ---
54: Your agent prompt here...
55: AGENT_EOF
56: ```
57: 
58: ### Adding a Skill
59: 
60: ```bash
61: # Create skill directory and file
62: mkdir -p .opencode/skills/my-skill
63: cat > .opencode/skills/my-skill/SKILL.md << 'SKILL_EOF'
64: ---
65: name: my-skill
66: description: Use when needing my skill
67: ---
68: # Instructions
69: SKILL_EOF
70: ```
71: 
72: ### Adding a Command
73: 
74: ```bash
75: # Create command file
76: cat > .opencode/commands/my-command.md << 'CMD_EOF'
77: ---
78: description: My custom command
79: agent: build
80: ---
81: Run my custom command with $ARGUMENTS
82: CMD_EOF
83: ```
84: 
85: ## Documentation
86: 
87: - [OpenCode Documentation](https://opencode.ai/docs)
88: - [Kilo Documentation](https://kilo.ai/docs)
89: - [Agent Skills Specification](https://agentskills.io)
90: 
91: ## License
92: 
93: MIT - See individual components for their respective licenses.

(End of file - total 93 lines)
</content>","metadata":{"preview":"# Just-OC: OpenCode/Kilo Mono Repo

Unified OpenCode and Kilo Code configuration repository for managing plugins, agents, skills, and commands in a mono repo structure.

## Quick Start

```bash
# Clone this repository
git clone <repo-url> ~/just-oc

# Symlink for OpenCode
ln -s ~/just-oc/.opencode ~/.config/opencode

# Symlink for Kilo Code
ln -s ~/just-oc/.opencode ~/.config/kilo/opencode
# OR
ln -s ~/just-oc/.opencode ~/.kilo/opencode

# Restart OpenCode/Kilo to load the new configuration
```","truncated":false,"loaded":[]},"title":"README.md","time":{"start":1775687647458,"end":1775687647475}}}}

{"type":"text","timestamp":1775687794579,"sessionID":"ses_290c2b92affe5BqpPE669VtEG4","part":{"id":"prt_d6f3d65a70019MyduXice50FSk","messageID":"msg_d6f3d474b001DrO6UsiZkeAAG3","sessionID":"ses_290c2b92affe5BqpPE669VtEG4","type":"text","text":"**Rejection**

This task is being rejected. It falls outside the <agent>test_engineer_worker</agent> archetype's scope of work.

**Reason for rejection**

The dispatch requests implementation of an authentication middleware in `src/middleware/auth.ts` — JWT validation logic, token extraction, error response construction, and edge case handling. These are server-side implementation tasks, not test authoring tasks. The <agent>test_engineer_worker</agent> archetype owns test strategy, oracle design, red-phase failing-test specification, and verification evidence collection. Writing authentication middleware logic is the <agent>backend_developer_worker</agent>'s lane.

The brief being detailed and well-specified does not make it a testing task. The use of dispatch terms like "write boundary" and "read-only context" are standard terms across all archetype briefs — they do not reclassify implementation work as testing work. The mention of existing test files (`tests/middleware/auth.test.ts`) does not make this a testing task either; the brief asks me to implement the middleware that those tests will exercise, not to author the tests themselves.

**Suggested archetype**

<agent>backend_developer_worker</agent> — this is server-side implementation work with contract-preserving logic writing.

**Acceptance criteria**

If this dispatch were restructured as a red-phase task — authoring failing tests that specify what the auth middleware should do — I could accept it. Specifically, if the objective became "author red-phase failing tests in `tests/middleware/auth.test.ts` that specify the expected behavior of the auth middleware," that would be within my archetype. As written, the objective is implementation, not test authoring.

**Confirmation**

No tests have been authored or modified. No production code has been authored or modified. This rejection is the complete and final response.","time":{"start":1775687794577,"end":1775687794577}}}
